headline_metrics.nii: decreased 2.8% QoQ, or $105 million; full-year 2026 growth guided to 2%-3% (down from 3%-4%)
headline_metrics.nco_rate: 61 basis points (up 4 bps QoQ); 2026 guidance ~55 bps

nim_nii.summary: NII fell 2.8% QoQ on two fewer days and seasonal deposit mix; full-year 2026 NII growth guidance was cut to 2%-3% from 3%-4% because the Fed funds rate is now expected to remain unchanged. NIM dipped 5 bps to 3.02% but full-year 2026 average NIM is still expected to exceed 2025's 3.03%.
nim_nii.quote: For full year 2026, we now expect net interest income to increase 2% to 3% compared with our prior expectation of 3% to 4% growth.
nim_nii.speaker: Michael Maguire, CFO

credit_normalization.summary: Asset quality remained strong; net charge-offs rose 4 bps QoQ to 61 bps and nonperforming loans rose 2 bps to 50 bps, with the consumer NPL increase driven by a change in indirect auto nonaccrual criteria rather than credit deterioration. Full-year NCO guidance unchanged at about 55 bps.
credit_normalization.quote: The increase in consumer nonperforming loans was primarily due to a change in the nonaccrual criteria for certain indirect auto loans, which we disclosed in our 10-K rather than any deterioration in underlying credit trends.
credit_normalization.speaker: Michael Maguire, CFO

cre_reserves.summary: Management did not disclose a specific CRE reserve build, release, or flat action this quarter; CRE was mentioned only as a contributor to wholesale loan growth and as a portfolio that improved within nonperforming loans. No explicit office CRE reserve commentary was given.

deposit_costs.summary: Average interest-bearing deposit costs fell 14 bps QoQ to 2.09% and total deposit costs fell 9 bps to 1.55%, though management flagged a competitive, yield-seeking environment with cumulative betas ticking up (IB beta 45% to 46%, total 30% to 31%).
deposit_costs.quote: Average interest-bearing deposit costs declined 14 basis points linked quarter to 2.09% and average total deposit costs declined 9 basis points to 1.55%.
deposit_costs.speaker: Michael Maguire, CFO

capital_return.summary: Truist raised its full-year 2026 buyback target to roughly $5 billion from $4 billion (after $1.1 billion repurchased in Q1, targeting $1.2 billion in Q2), with CET1 stable at 10.8%; the increase was driven by capital planning rather than Basel III, which could cut RWAs 9%-11%.
capital_return.quote: We are targeting repurchases of $1.2 billion in the second quarter and approximately $5 billion in 2026, compared with our previous expectation for $4 billion of repurchases for full year 2026.
capital_return.speaker: Michael Maguire, CFO

loan_growth.summary: Average loans rose 0.7% QoQ to $327 billion, driven by 1.8% commercial growth offset by a 0.9% consumer decline; management reaffirmed organic 3%-4% average loan growth for 2026 and stated M&A is not a priority, so growth is organic.
loan_growth.quote: Based on our current pipeline and economic outlook, we continue to expect average loan growth of approximately 3% to 4% in 2026.
loan_growth.speaker: Michael Maguire, CFO

macro_outlook.summary: Management framed the environment as competitive with higher-for-longer rates (no Fed cuts now assumed in 2026), citing net in-migration into its markets as a tailwind while acknowledging deposit-gathering pressure; overall tone was constructive on momentum but cautious on rates.
macro_outlook.quote: if rates higher for longer is a tough operating environment for deposits
macro_outlook.speaker: William Rogers, Chairman and CEO

notable_qa.summary: Erika Najarian (UBS) and Mike Mayo (Wells Fargo) pressed Bill Rogers on a Bloomberg article speculating Truist could be a sale/merger target and on succession; Rogers dismissed the rumor and repeatedly stated M&A is not a priority, emphasizing the standalone plan.
notable_qa.quote: We've been really clear on the M&A front, Mike. I mean I don't know how to be more clear that, that's just not a priority for us.
notable_qa.speaker: William Rogers, Chairman and CEO
